name: pod-monitor
description: PreToolUse(Bash) advisory hook that fires when a Bash command launches a GPU pod (`hexa cloud nohup` / `hexa cloud fire` / `hexa cloud run`). Emits non-blocking additionalContext reminding the agent of commons @D g57 — 🛰️ Monitor tool attached to the streaming log AND `SAVE_POD=1` envar set AND ⏱ walltime estimated upfront with pod-hours headroom AND 📋 per-project `./pods.json` ledger entry via `hexa cloud dispatch add` (cloud@0.4.0 + hexa-lang PR #1699). Closes the R8a-LOST class (pod terminated mid-run before result.json reached the operator) AND the ghost-pod class (caller fires but never registers in cwd's pods.json → billing/verdict tracking lost). Implemented in hexa-lang (`_pod_monitor.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). 0.1.4 — pods.json ledger hint added (caller MUST 'hexa cloud dispatch add <jid> <pid> <dir>' post-fire; hexa cloud fire/nohup does NOT auto-attach upstream-side). 0.1.3 — `_has_pod_fire` uses token-position match past env-var prefix (the cloud verb must SIT at command position, not be a substring of the cmd) so commit messages / grep patterns / docs that merely mention the phrase no longer trigger noise. `fire` verb (hexa-lang PR #1309) added to the match set. 0.1.5 — dead retired-rule source citation replaced with the current no-opt-out rule (project.tape @D s11).
